cuisine_type: Contemporary tasting menu — East-meets-West (Cantonese + Ningbo + Western technique)
address: Attic of a wine bar (Alea ground floor) — Jing'an district
head_chef: Zee Zheng (former Taian Table sous chef) + Charles Tan + Ricky — three-chef collaborative kitchen
restaurant_group: Independent (Zee Zheng also operates Alea, a casual Ningbo-inspired bistro on the ground floor below Fabula)
signature_dishes: seasonal tasting menu blending Cantonese, Ningbo, and Western techniques
vibe_and_space: Compact attic format above a wine bar (the wine bar = Alea, the casual sibling). Chef-counter seating with theatrical-flair plating. Multi-chef kitchen rather than single-toque format — distinguishes it from Taian Table's solo-chef paradigm. The Alea-downstairs / Fabula-upstairs vertical is itself a notable format experiment.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Shanghai's most institutionally-validated 2025 opening — Michelin one-star on first listing. The three-chef collaboration (Zee Zheng ex-Taian Table + Charles Tan + Ricky) is the key innovation; it produces an East-meets-West tasting menu that doesn't read like any single chef's signature voice. Critics flag this as 'when independent restaurants with no PR budget get recognized, the award means something.' Recommend for one-shot special occasion or date; the multi-chef format and theatrical plating make for a memorable experience. The downstairs sibling Alea offers a casual Ningbo bistro entry point if Fabula's price or reservation lead time doesn't work. Distinct from any other 2025 opening because the chef pedigree + Michelin recognition arrived simultaneously — most hot-new venues lack one or the other.
contradictions: Hot-new — Michelin authority is high but 大众点评 and long-form 公众号 review base is still thin. The three-chef format is uncommon in Chinese fine dining; sustainability of a no-single-headline-chef brand identity is the open question. Treat as recommendation-with-caveat until at least 3 长文 reviews land and 大众点评 ratings stabilize.
